backend.changelog: Pre-Generate close-foreign-docs prompt now runs BEFORE attempting to open the BOM root assembly, so leftover non-BOM documents that were blocking SolidWorks Connector's fresh root open get cleared first. 1.1.18 placed the prompt only in the post-engage recheck branch, which never fired when the root wasn't already loaded -- the root auto-open polled 3 minutes then aborted with 'SolidWorks is still loading...'. Also: the partial-close safety gate no longer falls through to the legacy 'close them manually' abort when even a single library / template doc lacks a 3DX plmId; the closeable subset goes through the modal as before and any leftover unmanaged docs surface via a post-close 'Closed X, Y remain' message. Both fixes applied at both prompt sites for consistency. Workstation Apply Update for CadBridge 1.8.47 still needed to benefit from the auto-close half.

cadBridge.changelog: New /api/cadbridge/close-docs endpoint accepts a PLMID list and closes those documents in SolidWorks via the IModelDocExtension.GetPLMID() match -- replaces backend 1.1.18/1.1.19 frontend's previous 'wait for user to manually close non-BOM docs' step with a one-click prompt. Defensive: agent re-reads GetSaveFlag right before CloseDoc(title) and skips any document that turned dirty between modal render and confirm (reason=modified). Matching is case-insensitive uppercased to align with the frontend's uppercase PLMID convention. Backwards-compatible: older frontends without the prompt continue working through the unchanged CloseAllDocuments path.
